result.markdown:
--- title: "Potyvirus Protein Motifs and Interaction Networks" subtitle: "Functional signature motifs across the polyprotein and the curated CI/HC-Pro/VPg interactome" author: "chrissi m." date: last-modified format: html: theme: cosmo toc: true toc-depth: 3 toc-location: left code-fold: true code-tools: true page-layout: full execute: echo: true warning: false message: false --- ## Overview The potyvirus genome is translated as a single polyprotein that is cleaved into **~11 mature proteins**, each carrying short functional signature motifs whose substitution changes transmissibility, silencing suppression, replication or movement [@revers2015]. This chapter (i) catalogues those motifs with their roles and primary references, (ii) locates the linear ones in the ICTV Potyviridae alignment [@inouenagata2022], and (iii) draws the curated protein–protein interaction network that centres on the cylindrical inclusion (CI) helicase — the protein carrying the DNAzyme-like locus studied in the earlier chapters. ## Signature motif catalogue ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} motifs <- tibble::tribble( ~protein, ~motif, ~pattern, ~role, ~ref, "P1", "protease", "—", "Serine protease; host-range adaptation", "revers2015", "HC-Pro", "KITC", "KITC", "Aphid transmission (N-terminal)", "valli2018", "HC-Pro", "FRNK", "FR[NR]K", "RNA-silencing suppression; symptom severity", "valli2018", "P3N-PIPO", "G2A6", "GA{5,7}", "Transcriptional slippage → PIPO; cell-to-cell movement", "chung2008", "CI", "Walker A", "G.{4}GK[ST]", "ATPase P-loop of the RNA helicase", "sorel2014", "CI", "DECH box", "DECH", "Helicase motif II (Mg2+ coordination)", "sorel2014", "6K2", "TM anchor", "—", "ER membrane anchor; replication vesicles", "revers2015", "VPg", "eIF4E-bind","—", "Genome-linked protein; binds host eIF4E (recessive resistance)", "robaglia2006", "NIa-Pro", "catalytic", "H.{31,35}D.{60,70}GXCG", "Main protease catalytic triad", "revers2015", "NIb", "GDD", "GDD", "RdRp catalytic motif (polymerase)", "revers2015", "CP", "DAG", "DAG", "Aphid transmission determinant (N-terminal)", "atreya1991" ) knitr::kable(motifs, caption = "Potyvirus mature-protein signature motifs, roles and primary references.") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Potyvirus mature-protein signature motifs, roles and primary references. |protein |motif |pattern |role |ref | |:--------|:----------|:----------------------|:--------------------------------------------------------------|:------------| |P1 |protease |— |Serine protease; host-range adaptation |revers2015 | |HC-Pro |KITC |KITC |Aphid transmission (N-terminal) |valli2018 | |HC-Pro |FRNK |FR[NR]K |RNA-silencing suppression; symptom severity |valli2018 | |P3N-PIPO |G2A6 |GA{5,7} |Transcriptional slippage → PIPO; cell-to-cell movement |chung2008 | |CI |Walker A |G.{4}GK[ST] |ATPase P-loop of the RNA helicase |sorel2014 | |CI |DECH box |DECH |Helicase motif II (Mg2+ coordination) |sorel2014 | |6K2 |TM anchor |— |ER membrane anchor; replication vesicles |revers2015 | |VPg |eIF4E-bind |— |Genome-linked protein; binds host eIF4E (recessive resistance) |robaglia2006 | |NIa-Pro |catalytic |H.{31,35}D.{60,70}GXCG |Main protease catalytic triad |revers2015 | |NIb |GDD |GDD |RdRp catalytic motif (polymerase) |revers2015 | |CP |DAG |DAG |Aphid transmission determinant (N-terminal) |atreya1991 | ::: ::: The motifs are backed by: HC-Pro KITC/FRNK [@valli2018]; the P3N-PIPO frameshift/movement locus [@chung2008]; the CI helicase Walker-A/DECH motifs and its interactome [@sorel2014]; VPg–eIF4E and recessive resistance [@robaglia2006]; the CP DAG aphid-transmission determinant [@atreya1991]; and the overall polyprotein organisation [@revers2015]. ## Locating linear motifs in the ICTV alignment ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} aln <- readAAStringSet(here::here("data", "OPSR.Poty.Fig3.v16_align.txt")) cons <- as.character(consensusString(aln, ambiguityMap = "X", threshold = 0.4)) cons_nogap <- gsub("-", "", cons) # Linear motifs searchable directly on the consensus (regex) scan_set <- dplyr::filter(motifs, pattern != "—") hits <- scan_set %>% dplyr::rowwise() %>% dplyr::mutate( match_pos = list(as.integer(gregexpr(pattern, cons_nogap, perl = TRUE)[[1]])), n_hits = sum(match_pos > 0), first_pos = ifelse(n_hits > 0, match_pos[1], NA_integer_)) %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>% dplyr::select(protein, motif, pattern, n_hits, first_pos) knitr::kable(hits, caption = "Signature motifs located on the consensus of the ICTV Potyviridae alignment (gap-free coordinates).") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Signature motifs located on the consensus of the ICTV Potyviridae alignment (gap-free coordinates). |protein |motif |pattern | n_hits| first_pos| |:--------|:---------|:----------------------|------:|---------:| |HC-Pro |KITC |KITC | 1| 297| |HC-Pro |FRNK |FR[NR]K | 1| 426| |P3N-PIPO |G2A6 |GA{5,7} | 0| NA| |CI |Walker A |G.{4}GK[ST] | 1| 1183| |CI |DECH box |DECH | 1| 1272| |NIa-Pro |catalytic |H.{31,35}D.{60,70}GXCG | 1| 2019| |NIb |GDD |GDD | 1| 2567| |CP |DAG |DAG | 1| 1614| ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} found <- dplyr::filter(hits, n_hits > 0) ggplot(found, aes(x = first_pos, y = reorder(paste(protein, motif), first_pos))) + geom_point(size = 3, colour = "#A23B72") + geom_segment(aes(xend = first_pos, yend = paste(protein, motif)), x = 0, colour = "grey80") + labs(title = "Approximate motif positions along the potyvirus polyprotein consensus", x = "Consensus position (aa, gap-free)", y = NULL) + cowplot::theme_cowplot() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](04b_potyvirus_motifs_files/figure-html/motif-scan-plot-1.png){width=864} ::: ::: ## Protein–protein interaction network A curated interaction network of the potyvirus proteins, centred on CI, drawn from the CI interactome review [@sorel2014], the HC-Pro multitasking review [@valli2018], the movement/PIPO literature [@chung2008] and VPg–host factor interactions [@robaglia2006]. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nodes <- tibble::tibble( id = c("P1","HC-Pro","P3","P3N-PIPO","6K1","CI","6K2","VPg","NIa-Pro","NIb","CP","eIF4E"), group = c(rep("viral", 11), "host")) nodes$label <- nodes$id nodes$title <- ifelse(nodes$group == "host", "Host translation factor", "Viral protein") edges <- tibble::tribble( ~from, ~to, ~process, ~ref, "VPg", "eIF4E", "translation/resistance", "robaglia2006", "VPg", "NIb", "replication", "revers2015", "NIa-Pro", "NIb", "replication", "revers2015", "CI", "HC-Pro", "movement", "sorel2014", "CI", "CP", "movement", "sorel2014", "CI", "P3N-PIPO", "movement", "chung2008", "CI", "VPg", "replication", "sorel2014", "HC-Pro", "HC-Pro", "self (dimer)", "valli2018", "HC-Pro", "CP", "aphid transmission", "valli2018", "CP", "CP", "encapsidation", "revers2015") edges$color <- dplyr::recode(edges$process, "movement" = "#2E86AB", "replication" = "#D62828", "translation/resistance" = "#8E44AD", .default = "#7f8c8d") edges$title <- paste0(edges$process, " [", edges$ref, "]") visNetwork(nodes, edges, height = "600px", width = "100%") %>% visGroups(groupname = "viral", color = "#f5b041") %>% visGroups(groupname = "host", color = "#82e0aa", shape = "diamond") %>% visEdges(smooth = FALSE) %>% visOptions(highlightNearest = TRUE, nodesIdSelection = TRUE) %>% visLegend() %>% visLayout(randomSeed = 42) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## CI as a hub The CI helicase is the most connected viral node — bridging replication (with VPg/NIb) and cell-to-cell movement (with CP, HC-Pro and P3N-PIPO) [@sorel2014]. It is also the protein carrying the conserved DNAzyme-like locus (Chapters on the DNAzyme motif and HGT) and the ELM-rich CI region (Protein Motif Analysis), making it the natural focal point where the sequence-level and network-level observations in this book converge.
